set.body: Add two read-only commands: one prints the stable identity key for any doc, the other walks docs/ and produces a machine-readable inventory of every document (type, state, relationships). Also writes the migration record explaining the metadata rollout. No behavior changes yet.
set.title: Phase 0: worklog wiki-key + worklog ia-inventory (read-only) + migration record docs/migrations/0002-ia-content-model.md

set.body: Backfill every doc with its stable identity key and a current-vs-historical state. Frozen docs (plans, snapshots, old reports) are never edited — their metadata goes in sidecar files; only sanctioned living docs get in-place frontmatter. After this, every page can say what it is and whether it is current. Review notes: decide the sidecar file layout up front (identity keys contain slashes, so sidecars need nested folders or encoded filenames), and item sidecars hold only extra links and provenance — item state always comes from the work log, never the sidecar.
set.title: Phase 1: worklog ia-normalize — sidecars for frozen docs, in-place for sanctioned-live; backfill wiki_key (ledger-seeded) + truth_state

set.body: Generate index pages so common questions have one landing spot: all decisions (ADRs and plans with supersede chains), all releases with what shipped in each, and an archive of status reports. Regenerated automatically at release and plan-capture.
set.title: Phase 3: generated indexes — Decisions, Releases, Status Archive; wire ia-index into release + plan-capture skills

set.body: Define one frontmatter schema that every doc type validates against, with per-type required fields, plus a small validator. Today four different frontmatter styles coexist and the roadmap has none, so tooling can't reliably read doc metadata.
set.title: Phase 0: schema/doc.schema.json unified frontmatter schema + stdlib validator (adr.schema.json pattern)

set.body: The wiki publisher reads a generated manifest that says exactly which pages belong on the wiki and how each renders, replacing the implicit publish list. The hand-maintained Home page is retired in favor of the generated one.
set.title: Phase 2: extend wiki-publish to consume publish-manifest.json; replace hand-maintained wiki-home.md with generated Home

set.body: CI checks that every doc has an identity key, no two docs share one, and frontmatter matches the schema. Warn-only for one release so the team can adjust, then failures block merges.
set.title: Phase 1: CI gates — wiki_key present/unique, schema-valid frontmatter (warn one cycle, then hard)

set.body: Generate the reader-facing wiki pages: a question-driven Home, a sidebar grouped into Current Truth vs History, and a banner on every page saying whether it is current, a snapshot, or superseded. Readers stop guessing from file names. Review caveat: pages published before this work will not pick up banners automatically because publishing skips unchanged sources — plan either a one-time re-publish tracked by a separate render hash, or scope banners to generated pages until a migration pass runs.
set.title: Phase 2: worklog ia-render + ia-manifest — generated Home, Sidebar, publish-time truth banners in docs/.index/rendered/

set.body: Build the traceability graph linking plans to work items to tickets, code changes, tests, and releases — browsable as a wiki page and validated by a checker that reports released work missing its evidence links. Suggested links are proposed for human confirmation, never auto-written.
set.title: Phase 4: ia-graph typed-edge taxonomy + link-pr + trace-check + Traceability Index; propose-only edge seeding via suggestions.jsonl

set.body: Synced tickets showed only an internal id instead of a human-readable description, so a PM or new developer opening an issue could not tell what it was about. Fix: a description field on work items, wired through the CLI, plan capture, and both authoring skills, plus descriptions backfilled onto all open tickets.
set.title: Tickets unreadable: no junior-dev/PM description — add --body to add/update, plan-capture task descriptions, skill rule, backfill open items

set.body: Nightly log compaction has failed for three nights, so old events are not being archived. Cause: a work item id was mistyped once, creating a ghost item with no creation record; when the compactor snapshots it, it fills in default fields the original never had, notices the result differs, and aborts to be safe. Fix: the compactor must write the item exactly as folded — defaults belong at creation time, not snapshot time — plus a regression test covering a closed ghost item.
set.resolution: fixed in PR #103 — fold snapshot branch no longer fabricates taxonomy defaults
set.title: Compaction aborts on closed orphan item — snapshot must not inject taxonomy defaults

set.body: Pages published to the GitHub wiki show the metadata block from the top of the source file as ugly raw text, because GitHub's wiki renderer does not understand it. Fix: the publish step strips that block before writing the wiki copy; repository files keep their metadata untouched. Already-published pages get one cleanup re-publish.
set.title: GitHub Wiki shows raw YAML frontmatter on published pages — strip at publish time

set.body: Adds a pr-description skill to the plugin so substantial PRs read as durable design records: product context, exact problem, runtime caller, file-by-file changes, enforced sequence, honest testing, deliberate out-of-scope, and a ticket glossary.
set.title: PR-description skill: 10-section high-context PR body spec (big picture, runtime context, scope, flow, testing honesty, out-of-scope, ticket glossary)

set.body: Issues become durable intent records, not disposable titles: every synced ticket carries summary, big-picture context, motivation, done-criteria, and a traceability section linking the frozen plan page, epic, ADRs, and spec sections the item implements.
set.title: issue-description skill + rich ticket bodies in ticket-sync (summary/context/outcome/scope/acceptance/traceability from the item graph)

set.body: The IA gates added to pre-commit import new modules during the hook, creating .pyc files after staging. A branch then commits them; the next merge into a clone whose hook regenerated the same files untracked aborts with would-be-overwritten. Fix: hooks run python with PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 so a hook can never dirty the worktree.
set.title: pre-commit hook pollutes worktree with bin/__pycache__ after git add — merge aborts on tracked-vs-untracked pyc collision
Repair frozen-plan drift: restore grok-compat-and-mermaid-viz to published version, move background-subagent rule to amendment doc
Add schema drift test: schema/doc.schema.json must stay equivalent to bin/ia.py validator constants
Schema boundary: split doc.schema.json (documents) from entity schema (items, releases, code-changes) — defer until a second graph entity needs validation
Author superseding UI plan: re-base wiki_ticket_sdd_ui design on the shipped IA & content model (manifest, wiki_key, truth_state, graph, sidecars)

set.body: Local read-only dashboard app (new public repo wiki_ticket_sdd_ui) visualizing any worklog repo: board, roadmap, activity, releases, docs, publish plane, sync health, charts, and an interactive traceability graph. Re-based on the shipped IA & content model: the UI consumes the committed docs/.index plane (inventory, graph, publish manifest) instead of deriving document metadata itself.
set.resolution: tracking moved to wiki_ticket_sdd_ui (epic #1, items #2-#9)
set.title: WikiTicket UI — IA-aware dashboard (supersedes wiki-ticket-ui)

build, and vitest. Unchanged from the superseded plan.

Dark glassy shell, left nav, top bar with repo/branch/tag/drift indicators, recent
repos remembered. Unchanged from the superseded plan.

set.body:
Docs browser consumes _inventory.json with truth-state badges and supersede chains.
Publish plane joins manifest + ledger + live re-hash into green/amber/red drift,
mirroring the wiki-publish skill's own two-hash rules. Releases links snapshots via
truth_state, not filename patterns.
set.resolution: tracking moved to wiki_ticket_sdd_ui (epic #1, items #2-#9)
set.title: Panels wave 2: Releases, Docs browser (inventory-driven), Publish plane (3-way drift), Sync health, Charts

set.body:
The original API surface plus thin reads of docs/.index/_inventory.json, _graph.json,
publish-manifest.json and a trace-check endpoint. Never re-derives worklog or IA
semantics: shells out to bin/worklog, reads committed index files.
set.resolution: tracking moved to wiki_ticket_sdd_ui (epic #1, items #2-#9)
set.title: Server: Hono JSON API over any worklog repo — fold, events, docs, index plane, git, gh, ledger, sync state

As in the superseded plan; Roadmap panel reads the YAML frontmatter header the IA
work gave docs/roadmap.md instead of HTML comments.

init.sh copy list missing IA modules (ia.py, ia_render.py, ia_graph.py, canonical.py, sync_dispatch.py) — fresh repos get worklog with broken ia-* commands
ia.classify treats README.md inside docs/plans|status|adr dirs as a doc of that type — generic repos with folder READMEs fail inventory
Add MIT LICENSE (repo was public with no license)
